---
title: "Guide Overview"
---

This guide takes you from your first table to advanced styling, interactivity, and Shiny integration. Pages are grouped into the same sections you see in the sidebar — read top-to-bottom for the curriculum, or jump to whichever section you need.

## Get going

| Page | What you'll learn |
|---|---|
| [Quick Start](quick-start.qmd) | Install tabviz, render your first table, layer in styling, themes, and conditional formatting |

## Structure — what the table contains

| Page | What you'll learn |
|---|---|
| [Column Types](columns.qmd) | Every `col_*()` and the four `viz_*()` columns, with formatting, alignment, and column groups |
| [Row Groups](row-groups.qmd) | Flat, hierarchical, and explicit collapsible sections via `group` and `web_group()` |
| [Titles & Labels](titles.qmd) | Title, subtitle, caption, footnote, axis label |
| [Split Plots](split-plots.qmd) | Sidebar navigation across subgroups via `split_by` / `split_table()` |

## Styling — colors, weights, themes

| Page | What you'll learn |
|---|---|
| [Row Styling](row-styling.qmd) | Bold, italic, color, indentation, badges; semantic row tokens |
| [Cell Styling](cell-styling.qmd) | Per-cell conditional formatting with formula expressions and the `.x` pronoun |
| [Themes](themes.qmd) | The seven presets, the input cascade (Tier 1 → 2 → 3), and `set_*()` modifiers |

## Working with built tables

| Page | What you'll learn |
|---|---|
| [Fluent API](fluent-api.qmd) | Pipe-based modifiers that work on `WebSpec`, htmlwidget, and Shiny proxy uniformly |
| [Recipes](recipes.qmd) | Composed snippets — significance highlighting, p-value formatting, reusable styling pipelines |

## Visualizations — embedded charts

| Page | What you'll learn |
|---|---|
| [Forest Plots](visualizations/forest-plots.qmd) | `viz_forest()` arguments, multi-effect rows, axis control, annotations |
| [Bar Charts](visualizations/bar-charts.qmd) | Magnitude comparisons across rows with `viz_bar()` |
| [Boxplots](visualizations/boxplots.qmd) | Median + spread + outliers with `viz_boxplot()` |
| [Violin Plots](visualizations/violin-plots.qmd) | Distribution shapes with `viz_violin()` |
| [Visualization Styling](visualizations/styling.qmd) | The marker-styling cascade across all `viz_*()` columns |

## Interaction & integration

| Page | What you'll learn |
|---|---|
| [Interactivity](interactivity.qmd) | Sort, filter, drag, in-place edit, tooltips |
| [Shiny](shiny.qmd) | Reactive tables and proxy-based incremental updates |

## Output

| Page | What you'll learn |
|---|---|
| [Export](export.qmd) | Save to SVG / PDF / PNG, paginate long tables, export split tables |
| [Package Data](package-data.qmd) | The five datasets shipped with the package, used throughout this guide |

## Two entry points

`tabviz()` is the general-purpose constructor. `forest_plot()` is a thin convenience wrapper for the common case where you only need point + lower + upper.

```r
# Full control
tabviz(data,
  label = "study",
  columns = list(
    col_text("category"),
    col_numeric("value"),
    viz_forest(point = "hr", lower = "lo", upper = "hi"),
    col_sparkline("trend")
  )
)

# Quick forest plot
forest_plot(data,
  point = "hr", lower = "lower", upper = "upper",
  label = "study",
  scale = "log", null_value = 1
)
```

:::{.callout-tip}
## When to reach for `forest_plot()`
Use `forest_plot()` when the table is "labels + a forest plot + maybe a CI string." The moment you need extra columns, badges, or column groups, switch to `tabviz()`.
:::

## Where else to look

- [Cheatsheet](../cheatsheet.qmd) — one-page lookup for functions and arguments.
- [Concepts](../concepts/index.qmd) — architecture, mental models, and the styling cascade.
- [Reference](../reference/index.qmd) — full API documentation.
- [Gallery](../gallery/index.qmd) — runnable examples by domain.
- [Troubleshooting](../reference/troubleshooting.qmd) — common issues and fixes.
